examples.title: TCP Three-Way Handshake Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates the SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK exchange between client and server with sequence numbers
examples.explanation: The handshake synchronizes initial sequence numbers (ISN) in both directions. Each side acknowledges the other's ISN + 1. This prevents accepting stale segments from previous connections.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - no significant memory allocation per exchange

examples.title: TCP Sliding Window Sender
examples.description: Implements the sender-side sliding window logic with send, ACK processing, and timeout handling
examples.code:
VARIABLES:
 sendBase // oldest unacknowledged byte
 nextSeqNum // next byte to send
 windowSize // receiver's advertised window
 cwnd // congestion window
 timer[] // one timer per unacked segment

FUNCTION sendData(data):
 while nextSeqNum < sendBase + windowSize AND nextSeqNum < sendBase + cwnd:
 segment = createSegment(seq=nextSeqNum, data)
 if sendBase == nextSeqNum:
 startTimer(sendBase)
 send(segment)
 nextSeqNum += len(data)

FUNCTION onACK(ackNum):
 if ackNum > sendBase:
 sendBase = ackNum
 cancelTimer()
 if sendBase < nextSeqNum:
 startTimer(sendBase)
 // Implements cumulative ACK - advances window

FUNCTION timeout():
 // Retransmit unacknowledged segment
 cwnd = 1 // Slow start after timeout
 retransmit(sendBase)
 startTimer(sendBase)
examples.explanation: Shows how TCP manages multiple unacknowledged segments. The window is bounded by both receiver's advertised window (flow control) and congestion window (congestion control). Cumulative ACKs advance sendBase, sliding the window forward.
examples.timeComplexity: O(windowSize) - can send up to window size segments initially
examples.spaceComplexity: O(windowSize) - must buffer unacknowledged segments for potential retransmission

examples.title: TCP Congestion Control Algorithms
examples.description: Implementation of Slow Start, Congestion Avoidance, and Fast Recovery state machines
examples.explanation: Implements classic TCP Reno congestion control. Note that timeout is more severe than duplicate ACKs (cwnd=1 vs cwnd=ssthresh/2). Modern algorithms like CUBIC modify these rules for high-speed networks.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per event - constant time calculations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - only tracking cwnd, ssthresh, and state
